steps.canonical_text: Suspect low blood sugar in a diabetic person who suddenly looks shaky, sweaty, pale, confused, weak, or unusually hungry.
If they are awake and can swallow safely, give about 15 grams of fast sugar: glucose tablets, half a cup of fruit juice, a sugary soft drink, or a spoon of sugar.
Wait about 15 minutes. If they still feel unwell or look the same, give another 15 grams of fast sugar.
Once they feel better, give a small snack with both carbohydrate and protein, such as a sandwich or crackers with cheese.

If they are unresponsive, seizing, or cannot swallow safely, do not give food or drink. Call emergency services now and place them on their side. If they have prescribed glucagon and you know how to use it, give it as directed.
